landing.title: Quest Management
landing.description: Browse public quests, compete on leaderboards, and track your speedrun records. Quest authors can also create and publish quests with a visual editor.
landing.authorGuide: Quest author? Read the workflow guide
landing.featureExploreDesc: Browse the full quest catalog. Filter by category, check completion stats, and find your next challenge on the server.
landing.featureCompeteDesc: Speedrun leaderboards, QP rankings, and personal bests. See how you stack up against other players.
landing.featureCreateDesc: Visual step editor, in-game testing, and a streamlined review workflow for quest authors.

settings.loginRequired: Please log in to access settings.
settings.mcLinkPrompt: Link your Minecraft account in the Account tab to see your rankings.
settings.mcLinkInstruction: To link your Minecraft account, run {command} on the Minecraft server to get a token, then paste it below.
settings.systemMapDesc: Configure the MTR System Map API to enable station and route autocomplete in the quest editor.
settings.noRankingData: No ranking data found. Complete some quests in-game to see your stats here.

ranking.title: Leaderboards
ranking.noSpeedrunData: No speedrun data for this quest.
ranking.noSpeedrunDataYet: No speedrun data for this quest yet.
ranking.searchPrompt: Search for a quest above to view its speedrun leaderboard.
ranking.playerNotFound: Player not found or failed to load.
ranking.disqualifyDesc: This will remove the completion from all leaderboards and deduct the awarded QP from the player. This action cannot be undone.
ranking.disqualifyPlaceholder: e.g. Exploiting map bug to skip checkpoint
ranking.takeItEasy: The quest system aims to guide exploration and sightseeing; the leaderboard is purely for fun. We recommend taking it easy, as intense speedrunning might ruin your gameplay experience.

quests.questCatalogDesc: Explore quests, view speedrun leaderboards, and find your next challenge.
quests.tryDifferent: Try a different search term or category.
quests.name: Name
quests.noQuestsMine: You don't have any quests yet. Create one to get started!

editor.failedToLoadBody: {id} could not be loaded. It may not exist or you may not have permission.
editor.questSavedPending: Saved. There are pending draft changes awaiting staff approval.
editor.mtrNoDataTooltip: No station data loaded — click to fetch
editor.noSystemMapUrlBody: Configure the System Map API URL in Settings first.
editor.pendingDraftAdmin: This quest has pending draft changes. You can review and promote them to make them live.
editor.pendingDraftAuthor: This quest has pending game logic changes awaiting admin review. Metadata changes are already live.
editor.promoteConfirmText: Are you sure you want to promote the draft changes to the live version? This will make the draft game logic immediately available to all players.
editor.deleteConfirmText: Are you sure you want to delete {name} ({id})? This action cannot be undone.
editor.questIdHint: Lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, underscores. No double underscores.
editor.stepFailureCondition: Step Failure Condition (Overrides global failure condition)
editor.teleportDesc: Triggered when a player warps or uses tpa.
editor.manualTriggerDesc: Describe how to manually trigger this step.
editor.acl_desc: By adding members to the ACL, you can collaborate on quests with your team.
editor.cannotDowngradeBody: Non-staff users cannot demote themselves from OWNER.
editor.cannotRemoveBody: Non-staff users cannot remove themselves from the ACL.
editor.aclNeedsOwner: ACL must have at least one OWNER.
editor.aclNeedsOwnerSave: ACL must have at least one OWNER. Add an owner before saving.
editor.importDesc: Paste quest JSON below. This will overwrite the current editor state.
editor.invalidJsonBody: Please check the JSON format and try again.
editor.failedToCopyBody: Please select and copy the text manually.
editor.reviewDesc: Comparing live (published) game logic with the current draft.
editor.noDiffChanges: Draft and live versions are identical. No changes to review.
editor.noStats: No stats available for this quest yet. Stats are generated after players complete the quest.
editor.stepAnalyticsDesc: Average and median duration for each step. The bar width represents relative time compared to the slowest step.
editor.qpAdjustmentDesc: Retroactively adjust the QP value for this quest. This will update all existing completion records and adjust every affected player's QP balance.
editor.permissionNoticeBody: If any player has completed this quest, contact a staff member to perform QP adjustments.
editor.confirmQpAdjustmentText: This will change the quest points for {name} from {from} to {to}.
editor.confirmQpAdjustmentDelta: Every player who has completed this quest will have their QP balance adjusted by {delta} QP per completion.
editor.reasonPlaceholder: Explain why this adjustment is needed...
editor.lineWidth: Line width — safe limit {safe}px (1920x1080, GUI Scale {scale})
editor.tooltipOverflow: Some lines may overflow at GUI Scale {scale}. Consider adding line breaks to wrap long text.
editor.excludeFirstStepHint1: It is recommended to set the first step as a designated starting point to make Quest timing fairer:
editor.excludeFirstStepHint2: An "Reach Area" condition, set within the range of a Warp point with the description text "Warp to XXX with /warp". Take care that if the global failure condition has TeleportDetection set, set this step's failure condition to Constant False.  (Step failure condition overrides global failure condition)
editor.excludeFirstStepHint3: An "Reach Area" condition, set at a visible station landmark, with a description text guiding the player to this landmark.
editor.excludeFirstStepHint4: A "Depart from Station" or "Depart on Specific Line" condition.

criterionType.RideLineCriterionDesc: Ride a train on the specified line.
criterionType.VisitStationCriterionDesc: Enter the station area. If on a train, it must stop at the station.
criterionType.RideToStationCriterionDesc: Enter the station area while on a train that stops at the station.
criterionType.RideLineToStationCriterionDesc: Enter the station area on a train of the specified line that stops at the station.
criterionType.RideFromStationCriterionDesc: Board any train at this station. Triggers when the train doors close.
criterionType.RideLineFromStationCriterionDesc: Board a train of a specific line at this station. Triggers when the train doors close.
criterionType.OverSpeedCriterionDesc: Exceed the speed limit while on a train.
criterionType.TeleportDetectCriterionDesc: Triggered when the player uses a teleport command.
criterionType.ConstantCriterionDesc: A condition that is always fixed to true or false.
criterionType.DescriptorDesc: Wraps a condition to provide custom display text.
criterionType.AndCriterionDesc: Completed only when all sub-conditions are met.
criterionType.OrCriterionDesc: Completed when any sub-condition is met.
criterionType.NotCriterionDesc: Completed only when the sub-condition is NOT met.
criterionType.SequenceCriterionDesc: All sub-conditions must be met in order.
criterionType.ManualTriggerCriterionDesc: Completed by triggering a specific event ID via command blocks.
criterionType.LatchingCriterionDesc: Once met, it remains completed even if the condition is no longer met later.
criterionType.RisingEdgeAndConditionCriterionDesc: Another condition must also be met at the exact moment this condition becomes met.

admin.staffOnly: Only staff members can manage categories.
admin.deleteCategoryConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete category {id}? Quests using this category will have their category set to null.
admin.name: Name

guide.title: Workflow Guide
guide.description: NQuest Studio manages the full lifecycle of server quests, from drafting to publishing. This guide walks you through how quests move from your editor to the live game.
guide.questStatesDesc: Every quest exists in one of three visibility states. Understanding them is key to the publishing workflow.
guide.privateDesc: Only visible to the quest creator. This is the default state for new quests.
guide.privateDetail: Use this stage to draft and iterate without affecting anyone else.
guide.stagingDesc: Visible to all builders. Available for collaborative testing and peer review.
guide.stagingDetail: Builders can access staging quests in-game with debug mode enabled.
guide.publicDesc: Live for all players on the server. Only LPS staff can promote to this state.
guide.publicDetail: Once public, updates go through a separate review cycle.
guide.stateFlowNote: (Only LPS staff can promote to Public)
guide.creatingNewQuestDesc: Follow these steps to create, test, and publish a brand-new quest.
guide.step1Desc: Use the visual quest editor to build your quest with steps and criteria. Your quest starts in Private state, visible only to you.
guide.step2Desc: Join the Minecraft server and use <command>/nquest debugMode</command> to preview your quest. Make sure to link your Minecraft account first (see below).
guide.step3Desc: When you are satisfied with your quest, promote it from Private to Staging. This makes it visible to all builders for peer testing. Then ask a staff member to review it.
guide.step4Desc: A staff member reviews your quest. Once approved, they promote it from Staging to Public, making it live for all players.
guide.updatingPublishedQuestDesc: Already-published quests follow a safe update cycle so players are never disrupted.
guide.step5Desc: Make your changes to the quest steps. The quest will be marked as "Pending Review" automatically.
guide.step6Desc: While your changes are under review, players continue to see the original published version. Builders with debug mode can preview the updated version as a separate Staging quest.
guide.step7Desc: A staff member reviews your changes. Once approved, they promote the update, seamlessly replacing the old version for all players.
guide.tipsSetupDesc: A few things to set up before you start building quests.
guide.linkMcDesc: Run <command>/idtoken</command> in-game to get a token, then paste it in <settings>Settings</settings> to connect your Minecraft account. This is required for Private quest testing.
guide.debugModeDesc: Use <command>/nquest debugMode</command> in-game to toggle debug mode. When active, you can see and test quests in Private and Staging states without affecting the experience for regular players.
guide.liveSyncDesc: The Minecraft server automatically pulls quest data from NQuest Studio. After you save changes in the editor, your quest becomes available in-game within moments. No manual file transfers or JSON wrangling needed.
